title: Hybrid Quantum GAN Drug Discovery
tagline: Quantum-inspired generative AI for de novo molecule design
description: An end-to-end computational drug discovery platform that combines hybrid quantum-classical generative adversarial networks with classical ML for molecular generation, property prediction, toxicity screening, and docking.
problem: Traditional drug discovery is slow and expensive. Generative models can propose novel molecules, but without validity, property, and safety filters their output is not actionable for chemists.
solution: A modular pipeline where an HQ-MolGAN generates candidate SMILES, multi-model QSAR and toxicity stacks score them, AutoDock Vina estimates binding affinity, an RL agent optimizes for target properties, and SHAP explains the decisions — all surfaced through a Streamlit workflow with PDF reporting.
architecture: Streamlit UI → HQ-MolGAN generator → QSAR ensemble (RF/XGB/MLP/GNN) + toxicity classifiers → AutoDock Vina docking → RL property optimization → SHAP explainability → PDF reports. Data flows from QM9 and Tox21 download/preprocess scripts into checkpointed training modules.
features: Multi-model QSAR ensemble for property prediction
SHAP explainability and automated PDF reports
challenges: Keeping a 90+ dependency cheminformatics stack reproducible across CPU-only macOS
Balancing molecular validity with novelty in the GAN objective
Making optional native binaries (Vina) degrade gracefully
learnings: Designing reproducible scientific ML pipelines with pinned environments
Bridging quantum circuit simulation (PennyLane/Qiskit) with classical deep learning
Structuring training vs. UI concerns for research-grade tools
futureScope: GPU worker fleet with a job queue for at-scale generation
Containerized docking and dataset versioning (DVC / W&B)

title: SkinCare AI
tagline: Dual vision transformers + an LLM copilot for skin lesion screening
description: SkinCare AI classifies skin lesions as benign vs. malignant using fine-tuned DINOv2 and ResNet50 models, paired with a Groq-powered LLM medical chat, prediction history, and a feedback loop.
problem: Early skin cancer screening support is hard to access, and raw model probabilities are difficult for non-experts to interpret.
solution: An API-first FastAPI service exposes dual model selection (ViT vs. CNN), image-upload prediction, and an LLM assistant that explains results in patient-friendly language, with history, metrics dashboards, and feedback capture for continuous improvement.
architecture: Static frontend → FastAPI (/predict, /chat, /feedback, /history, /metrics) → dual model layer (DINOv2 + ResNet50) and LangChain/Groq with thread memory → CSV + image persistence.
features: Dual model selection between DINOv2 ViT and ResNet50 CNN
Image-upload malignancy prediction with sigmoid thresholding
AI medical assistant chat powered by Groq
Prediction history with a metrics dashboard
User feedback loop with saved images
challenges: Serving two large vision models behind a single low-latency API
learnings: Comparing ViT vs. CNN architectures on the same medical task
Building API-first ML products with feedback loops

title: RXShield AI
tagline: Real-time prescription compliance intelligence monorepo
description: RXShield AI is a production-style prescription compliance platform built as a multi-service monorepo: a Next.js frontend, an Express/TypeScript/Prisma backend on MongoDB, and a separate FastAPI risk engine.
problem: Prescription compliance checks are fragmented and hard to scale, and combining web, API, and ML scoring concerns in one codebase invites coupling.
solution: A decoupled monorepo with shared TypeScript types: the frontend talks to an Express API, which delegates scoring to a dedicated FastAPI risk-engine, with Docker Compose for local Mongo/Redis and documented deployment and security posture.
architecture: frontend (Next.js) → backend (Express /api/v1) → risk-engine (FastAPI /score), backed by MongoDB + Redis (BullMQ workers), wired together with a shared-types package.
features: Prisma/MongoDB data layer with seed workflows
challenges: Coordinating three services with consistent contracts
Designing for split deploys (Vercel + Atlas + Redis)
Moving queue workers off serverless for BullMQ
learnings: Monorepo architecture with shared type packages
Documenting environment matrices for production readiness
Separating ML scoring as an independent service
futureScope: Kubernetes deployment for the risk engine
Event-driven workers with a managed queue

title: Ecommerce Recommendation System
tagline: Neural collaborative filtering + Q-learning hybrid recommender
description: A full-stack e-commerce demo with a hybrid recommendation engine combining Neural Collaborative Filtering and Q-Learning, with behavior tracking, cart, and a Streamlit storefront.
problem: Static recommenders ignore real-time behavior, while pure collaborative filtering struggles to adapt rewards to user intent signals.
solution: A FastAPI backend serves an NCF model for collaborative signals and a Q-learning agent that shapes rewards from views (+1), cart adds (+5), and purchases (+10), exposed through a Streamlit storefront via REST.
architecture: Streamlit → FastAPI → Data Manager / Recommender (NCF MLP + Q-table) → CSV stores; models persisted via pickle and behavior logged for online updates.
challenges: Blending offline CF with online RL updates
Designing reward signals that reflect real intent
Keeping inference fast over a REST boundary
learnings: Recommender systems design end to end
Serving ML models through clean APIs

title: MRI Multimodal Epilepsy GNN
tagline: Graph fusion of EEG + MRI for drug-resistant epilepsy
description: A graph-based multimodal framework for drug-resistant epilepsy detection that fuses EEG functional-connectivity graphs and MRI structural graphs with a Graph Attention Network and fusion MLP.
problem: Single-modality models miss complementary signal; epilepsy decision support benefits from combining electrophysiology and structural imaging.
solution: Separate GAT encoders process EEG (19×512) and MRI region graphs (32×4), their embeddings are concatenated and classified, with a Streamlit UI and CLI inference over trained artifacts.
architecture: EEG → GAT encoder; MRI regions → GAT encoder; concatenated embeddings → classifier head. Artifacts: epilepsy_multimodal_model.pt and epilepsy_artifacts.joblib.
features: Packaged trained weights for reproducible demos
challenges: Constructing connectivity graphs from heterogeneous signals
Aligning modalities with different shapes and scales

title: Pharmaceutical Regulatory Compliance Agent
tagline: Enterprise RAG agent for pharma regulatory compliance
description: An AI-powered pharma regulatory compliance system: Phase 1 ingests policies (PDF → chunks → ChromaDB), Phase 2 runs a rule-based + RAG compliance agent, and Phase 3 ships a FastAPI web demo.
problem: Regulatory review is manual and error-prone, and pure LLM answers lack the grounded citations that compliance demands.
solution: A clause-aware ingestion pipeline builds a vector index; the agent combines deterministic YAML rules with retrieval to return structured violations that cite the exact regulation passages, served through a FastAPI demo UI.
architecture: Notebooks 01_policy_ingestion → 04_retrieval_test build the index; compliance_agent.py runs the agent; backend/main.py serves Phase 3; config/rules.yaml holds deterministic rules.
features: Test suite for the compliance agent
challenges: Grounding LLM output to avoid hallucinated rules
Chunking policy PDFs while preserving clause context
Keeping frozen Phase 1 artifacts immutable across phases

title: Soil Contamination Detection
tagline: GeoML for soil contamination and crop guidance
description: A Streamlit application for soil contamination and metal-concentration analysis from spectral / remote-sensing-style inputs, combining XGBoost, TensorFlow, and Groq LLM narration with district-specific models.
problem: Soil quality varies sharply by location and raw model outputs are hard for farmers and officials to act on.
solution: District-specific trained models score contamination, plotly visualizes the results, and a Groq LLM turns predictions into interpretable agronomic guidance in a single dashboard.
architecture: Streamlit (src/app.py) loads per-location .pkl models → XGBoost + TensorFlow inference → plotly visualizations → Groq LLM narrative layer.
challenges: Turning numeric predictions into trustworthy guidance

title: BookMyReels
tagline: TypeScript full-stack booking product with payments
description: BookMyReels.com is a booking platform with a React 19 + Vite + Tailwind frontend and an Express + TypeScript + MongoDB backend, with a Cashfree payments integration path.
problem: Booking products need a robust, typed backend with real auth and payments, not a prototype that breaks under production tooling.
solution: A production-grade Express/TypeScript API with MongoDB, auth, and full build/test/lint/typecheck tooling, paired with a fast React 19 Vite frontend for booking workflows.
architecture: React 19 (Vite) frontend → Express/TypeScript API (Mongoose, bcrypt, compression) → MongoDB; payments via Cashfree; tested with Jest.
features: Production build / test / lint / typecheck scripts
challenges: End-to-end type safety across the stack

title: Deepfake Detection
tagline: EfficientNet-B4 deepfake detection API
description: A production-style deepfake face-detection API using EfficientNet-B4 with an optional ViT hybrid for media authenticity screening.
problem: Synthetic media is increasingly convincing and needs automated authenticity screening at the API level.
solution: A FastAPI inference service wraps an EfficientNet-B4 classifier (with optional ViT) to score faces for manipulation, ready to embed in larger moderation pipelines.
architecture: Client → FastAPI (api/main.py) → EfficientNet-B4 (timm) inference → authenticity score.

title: Autism Screening
tagline: Form-based autism screening with TensorFlow
description: A Flask + TensorFlow/Keras screening tool that predicts autism likelihood from AQ-style questionnaire fields, with encoded categorical inputs.
problem: Early screening questionnaires produce data that is hard to score consistently without a model.
solution: A Flask app collects AQ-style form fields, applies saved encoders, and runs a Keras model to return a screening indication.
architecture: Flask form → encoders/ transforms → Keras model inference → screening result.

title: Externify
tagline: Production with an AI matching microservice
description: The primary Externify codebase: an AI service (FastAPI, spaCy, sentence-transformers) plus backend/frontend folders and extensive production documentation for AWS deployment, security, and testing.
problem: Startup products need production deployment and security process, not just feature code.
solution: A multi-service monorepo with a dedicated AI microservice and documented AWS Elastic Beanstalk deployment, security checklists, and a testing guide.
architecture: Frontend → Node backend → FastAPI AI service → PostgreSQL/RDS, deployed on AWS Elastic Beanstalk with JWT auth patterns.

title: Quantum-Enhanced Chest X-Ray
tagline: Text-to-chest-X-ray diffusion with a PQC text encoder
description: Text-to-chest-X-ray generation using diffusion models with a post-quantum-cryptography text-encoder narrative and multiple deployment entry points.
problem: Medical image synthesis needs both quality and a forward-looking security story for sensitive prompts.
solution: A diffusion pipeline generates chest X-rays from text, explored alongside PQC-enhanced text encoding, with a Streamlit demo and a Hugging Face deployment path.
architecture: Text prompt → (PQC) text encoder → diffusion model → generated X-ray; Streamlit and Hugging Face entry points.

title: AD-14 Injury Severity CNN
tagline: Injury type/severity detection with hospital routing
description: A CNN-driven system that detects injury type and severity from images and recommends hospitals based on the predicted severity for emergency response.
problem: Emergency triage needs fast severity assessment linked to appropriate care routing.
solution: An injury-severity CNN trained on multi-region trauma datasets feeds a recommendation layer that maps severity classes to hospital options.
architecture: Image upload → CNN classifier → severity class → hospital recommendation layer.

title: Li-Ion Battery SOH Prediction
tagline: NASA battery data pipeline with a CNN-LSTM baseline
description: A NASA battery-data pipeline performing cycle segmentation and ICA/DCIR/SOH feature extraction with a CNN-LSTM baseline, with a documented phased roadmap.
problem: Battery state-of-health prediction needs clean cycle data and a reproducible modeling pipeline.
solution: A config-driven pipeline ingests NASA .mat data, segments cycles, extracts features, and trains a CNN-LSTM baseline with a clear path to multimodal fusion.
architecture: NASA .mat → cycle segmentation → feature extraction (ICA/DCIR/SOH) → CNN-LSTM baseline; YAML-configured training.

title: Anemia ML Pipeline
tagline: End-to-end anemia analytics with SHAP and a Flask app
description: A full anemia analytics pipeline covering imputation, statistical tests, modeling, SHAP explainability, and reporting, with a Flask app for single and batch prediction.
problem: Clinical analytics needs both predictive power and interpretable, reportable results.
solution: A reproducible pipeline handles imputation and statistical testing, trains models, explains them with SHAP, generates reports, and serves predictions through Flask.
architecture: Data → imputation → stats tests → modeling → SHAP → reports; Flask app for single/batch predict.

title: NeuroWellness AI
tagline: Hybrid rule + Random Forest wellness screening
description: A mental-wellness screening tool from lifestyle inputs that blends rule-based risk with a Random Forest model in a Streamlit UI.
problem: Wellness screening benefits from combining transparent rules with learned patterns.
solution: A hybrid scorer weights rule-based logic, ML predictions, and trend signals (50/30/20) and logs results for trend tracking in a Streamlit interface.
architecture: Streamlit inputs → hybrid logic (rules + Random Forest + trend) → logged results.

title: Flood Detection (AP/Telangana)
tagline: Flood prediction and early warning with FastAPI
description: A flood prediction and early-warning system using merged meteorological datasets, with a FastAPI + SQLAlchemy backend and a React frontend.
problem: Flood-prone regions need accessible early-warning tools backed by real data.
solution: A FastAPI backend with SQLAlchemy persistence serves ML predictions from merged meteorological data to a React frontend.
architecture: Meteorological data → ML model → FastAPI (SQLAlchemy) → React frontend.

title: Super AI Prompts (MERN)
tagline: Versioned prompt vault with a secure admin
description: A premium AI prompt vault with a React Vite frontend and an Express/MongoDB backend featuring a versioned API and a secure admin seed.
problem: Curated prompt libraries need versioning and secure administration.
solution: A MERN application exposes a versioned API for prompts with a secure admin seed and a fast Vite frontend.
architecture: React (Vite) → Express versioned API → MongoDB; secure admin seed flow.

title: ProjectKaro
tagline: Next.js marketing platform for student project services
description: ProjectKaro is an SEO-oriented Next.js 14 marketing website for student project services, with contact/project forms and nodemailer-based lead capture.
problem: Service businesses need a fast, SEO-friendly site that converts visitors into leads.
solution: A componentized Next.js 14 marketing site with modular landing sections and nodemailer email capture, instrumented with Vercel speed insights.
architecture: Next.js 14 app → modular components (CTA/FAQ/testimonials) → nodemailer email capture.

title: Founderflow
tagline: Founder community platform
description: A founder/community platform with a React UI and a Node/Express backend covering events, founders, messages, and tickets.
problem: Founder communities need a single place for events, networking, and support.
solution: A React frontend over a Node/Express backend models founders, events, messages, and tickets.
architecture: React (Radix UI) → Node/Express backend → entity models (events/founders/messages/tickets).

title: Event Management System
tagline: Event and member management with leaderboards
description: An event and member management platform with an Express + MongoDB backend and a Vite React frontend featuring auth, events, and a leaderboard.
problem: Member organizations need event management with engagement tracking.
solution: A Vite React frontend with protected routes sits over an Express/MongoDB backend handling auth, events, and leaderboards.
architecture: Vite React (protected routes) → Express API → MongoDB; events and leaderboard modules.

title: Pothole Detection & Route Optimization
tagline: AI pothole detection with route optimization
description: A Streamlit app for AI pothole detection and route optimization, using OpenCV for vision and ReportLab for reporting.
problem: Road maintenance needs automated detection and efficient routing for repairs.
solution: A Streamlit app detects potholes from imagery and optimizes inspection/repair routes, exporting PDF reports.
architecture: Image input → OpenCV detection → route optimization → ReportLab PDF.

title: Aero Shot Media (Drone WebGL)
tagline: Scroll-driven WebGL drone cinematic experience
description: A single-page, scroll-driven WebGL drone cinematic experience built with Three.js, React Three Fiber, and GSAP.
problem: Brand sites for creative studios need an immersive, memorable experience.
solution: A scroll-driven 3D drone flythrough choreographed with GSAP and rendered with React Three Fiber for a cinematic landing.
architecture: React 19 + R3F scene → GSAP scroll timeline → Three.js rendering.
